summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates git identity, GitHub credentials metadata, and host info to non-publisher Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and harvests installer-side identity data: it reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, the local repo's.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI auth file), and collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), and CI environment variables. The collected data is JSON-encoded and POSTed over HTTPS to npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app, a Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to the declared homepage (ai-sdk.guide) or repository (ai-sdk-guide/ai-sdk-helpers). Comments in the script claim the data is anonymized/hashed, but the request body sends raw scmEmail, githubLogin/Email, hostname, and username fields. Every installer of this package leaks personally-identifying SCM identity and machine information to an attacker-controlled destination at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
